member.name: Mr Angus MacDonald
member.role: MP for Inverness, Skye and West Ross-shire
member.synopsis: Mr Angus MacDonald is the Liberal Democrat MP for Inverness, Skye and West Ross-shire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Scots Gaelic Language
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department has held recent discussions with the Scottish Government on the creation of a dedicated work visa for fluent Gaelic speakers to fill Gaelic-medium posts in Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We do not limit access to Gaelic-medium posts or any other roles that require Gaelic speakers, provided they meet the other requirements of the Skilled Worker route.We have therefore not had discussions relating to having specific routes for Gaelic speake...

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Scots Gaelic Language
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, pursuant to the Answer of 2 June 2026 to Question 3737, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential merits of introducing a dedicated visa route for other roles that require Gaelic
writtenQuestions.answerText: We do not limit access to Gaelic-medium posts or any other roles that require Gaelic speakers, provided they meet the other requirements of the Skilled Worker route.We have therefore not had discussions relating to having specific routes for Gaelic speake...

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Industry
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to help reduce industrial energy costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the pressure high energy costs place on businesses. Our Clean Power by 2030 mission will bring down bills, reducing exposure to volatile fossil fuel markets and the risk of carbon leakage. In the near term, we are acting through ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Russia: Supply Chains
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether she has had discussions with her Irish counterpart on reports that Aughinish Alumina has been linked to supply chains supporting Russia's defence industry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK and Irish governments regularly discuss our staunch support for countering Russia's aggression in Ukraine, including international efforts to maximise pressure on the Russian war economy. We conduct similar conversations with the EU on a regular ba...

writtenQuestions.heading: Business
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to improve the UK's business environment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is focused on creating a stronger business environment by making the UK the best place to start and grow a business, with a culture that supports entrepreneurship.Through the Small Business Plan, we are tackling late payments, improving ac...

writtenQuestions.heading: Poultry: Animal Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps his department plans to take in response to the consultation on the use of cages and crates for laying hens which closed on 9 March 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is analysing consultation responses and will publish a formal response in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: Excise Duties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she has made an assessment of whether Vehicle Excise Duty rates for vehicles in the highest bands are a proportionate application of the polluter pays principle.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Vehicle Excise Duty (VED) is a tax on vehicles used or kept on public roads. Different VED rates apply to cars, vans, and motorcycles, and the rate for each vehicle is calculated according to a range of factors, such as its date of first registration, wei...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: Excise Duties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the proportionality of Vehicle Excise Duty rates for higher-emission vehicles; and whether she plans to review the differential between vehicles in the lowest and highest emission ban
writtenQuestions.answerText: Vehicle Excise Duty (VED) is a tax on vehicles used or kept on public roads. Different VED rates apply to cars, vans, and motorcycles, and the rate for each vehicle is calculated according to a range of factors, such as its date of first registration, wei...

writtenQuestions.heading: Poultry: Animal Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent discussions he has had with The Humane League UK on the banning of cages for laying hens.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has had wide ranging discussions with animal welfare organisations and other stakeholders on the banning of cages for laying hens.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Scots Gaelic Language
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether the Home Office has considered the potential merits of creating a dedicated Gaelic Speaker Work Visa, allowing fluent Gaelic speakers from overseas to fill Gaelic-medium posts in Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Teachers, including those who deliver Gaelic-medium education, already have preferential treatment in the Skilled Worker immigration route, with salary requirements based on pay scales in each of the four UK nations. There is no need for a separate dedica...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Billing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his department has made of the potential merits of rebalancing policy costs currently levied on electricity bills across gas and electricity bills equally.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that policy costs fall disproportionately on electricity bills and are taking forward work to ensure that prices reflect the cheaper wholesale price of clean energy.On the distribution of costs between electricity and gas, the Go...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Heating Oil: Price Caps
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his department has made of the potential merits of introducing a price cap on domestic heating oil for households that are not connected to the gas grid.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has announced its intention to introduce new consumer protections in the heating oil market. The Competition and Markets Authority is currently examining how the market is working for consumers, with its final report expected in June.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Standing Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment her department has made of the potential impact of VAT on domestic energy bills on regional inequalities in electricity standing charges and unit rates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to lowering the cost of standing charges and have worked constructively with the regulator, Ofgem, on this issue. At the same time, the Government has taken significant steps to support households with the cost of energy while ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Standing Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his department has made of the potential merits of equalising standing charges for electricity consumers across the United Kingdom.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Standing charges vary by region because the costs of the transmission and distribution networks differ from place to place; a ‘common tariff’ for the most isolated areas would smooth out those higher local costs so customers there avoid very high prices, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.answerText: Standing charges vary by region because the costs of the transmission and distribution networks differ from place to place; a ‘common tariff’ for the most isolated areas would smooth out those higher local costs so customers there avoid very high prices, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Community Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what recent estimate his Department has made of the amount of community benefits paid to a) the United Kingdom, b) Scotland and c) the Scottish Highlands in 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Using the Scottish Register of Community Benefits and Shared Ownership, DESNZ estimates that the amount of community benefits paid in Scotland was approximately £30 million in 2025 of which £7 million was paid to communities in the Scottish Highlands. Com...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: Equipment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that workers injured through contact with construction equipment have a legal right to access service, maintenance, and operational records relating to tha
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Business and Trade recognises the importance of ensuring that workers injured through contact with construction equipment are able to access relevant support.The Construction Leadership Council (CLC) launched its Health Safety and Wellb...

writtenQuestions.heading: Industrial Accidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what conversations her department has had with the Health and Safety Executive with regards to their policy on verifying the accuracy of RIDDOR reports submitted by employers following workplace inciden
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations (RIDDOR) 2013 specify that the duty to make reports and follow the reporting procedure rests with the Responsible Person, as defined, which is often an employer. The reports must be...

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, with reference to her Department's policy paper entitled Tax avoidance loan schemes and the loan charge, published on 26 November 2025, if she will publish a breakdown of the sources of the £3.2 billion figure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government recognised that concerns continued to be raised about the loan charge and that some felt strongly that it had not been handled appropriately. The Government therefore commissioned an independent review of the loan charge to bring the matte...

writtenQuestions.heading: Timber: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she has plans to establish a cross-government taskforce to develop a strategy for reducing the United Kingdom's dependence on imported timber and strengthening domestic supply.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government recognises the growing global demand for timber. The Environmental Improvement Plan 2025 and the relaunched Timber in Construction Roadmap (2025) sets out our commitment to increase the supply and use of domestic timber. This includes work...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
